title: TAM / SAM / SOM
definition: Market sizing framework: total market, serviceable segment, and realistically obtainable share.
example: TAM = all global project management software ($50B), SAM = SMB teams in EU ($8B), SOM = 2% share in year 3 ($160M) — grounding a pitch in realistic reach.
whenToUse: Pitching new initiatives, prioritizing bets, investor conversations.
interviewPrompts: How would you size the market for X?

title: Opportunity Assessment
definition: Structured evaluation of problem severity, market size, strategic fit, and feasibility.
example: You score a 'AI meeting notes' bet: strong user pain (4/5), medium market ($2B SAM), high strategic fit, but engineering feasibility blocked until Q3 — recommend delayed pursue.
whenToUse: Go/no-go before major discovery or build investment.
interviewPrompts: Walk me through how you'd assess this opportunity.

title: Bottom-Up Market Sizing
definition: Calculating market size from unit economics: customers × price × purchase frequency.
example: 50,000 target clinics × 30% adoption × $200/mo subscription = $36M annual revenue potential — more defensible than citing a generic industry report.
whenToUse: When you need defensible numbers for stakeholders.
frameworkSteps: Multiply by ARPU or deal size
